source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/41645.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
description: Giacca in jersey ; monopetto; revers a uomo; spalle importanti di linea arrotondata. Vestibilità asciutta al punto vita, sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale;lunghezza ai fianchi; allacciatura a un bottone; tasche rettangolari applicate; cintura dello stesso tessuto, con profilo e fibbia ricoperti in faille di seta. Gonna in faille di seta e jersey di lana accoppiati e impunturati ; struttura a due teli,linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio e cinturetta in vita. T-shirt in jersey di lana; girocollo bordato (cm 1) in faille di seta; maniche corte; lunghezza ai fianchi;punto vita sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale; chiusura con zip sul dorso. Stola in jersey e faille abbinati e impunturati; composta da un grande rettangolo i cui lati corti sono ripiegati verso il centro e fissati in modo da creare un passaggio per le braccia.
materials: Faille di seta grigio (Clerici Tessuto, Como ; art.01404, col. 413); jersey melange grigio (Lanapon/Sisan, Milano ; art. 10718, col 120, 100% lana)
remark: Interessante è l’equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility “fashion” degli anni ‘40 e i canoni degli ‘80. L’essere un capo couture è dato dall’effetto di opulenza , evidenziato ai lati della silhouette e sul dietro, suggerito dalla stola.
working_process: Impunture orizzontali, parallele a 1 cm, a copertura totale sui due tessuti - jersey e seta - abbinati.
llm_description: Sketch of a jacket with wide lapels and a skirt, featuring a belt at the waist.

remark: Interessante è l’equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility “fashion” degli anni ‘40 e i canoni degli ‘80. L’essere un capo couture è dato dall’effetto di opulenza , evidenziato ai lati della silhouette e sul dietro, suggerito dalla stola.
working_process: Impunture orizzontali, parallele a 1 cm, a copertura totale sui due tessuti - jersey e seta - abbinati.
llm_description: Gray skirt suit featuring a jacket with wide, voluminous sleeves and a fitted skirt, accessorized with matching gloves and a headpiece.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/41673.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Coat in gray/black zigzag wool. Knee-length kimono style cut with hood, capacious sleeves, slanted besom pockets (4 cm). Wool/silk edging on hood and front. Tunic in white silk georgette. Straight 7/8 cut with wide round neck, short sleeves. White jet beading all over. Skirt in wool/silk satin with gorgeous sheen. Tubular style, knee length.
materials: Zigzag pattern wool (Distel, Paris; art. 960, col. 561; 100% wool), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. black; 66% wool, 27% silk, 7% nylon), silk georgette (Clerici Tessuto, Como), crystal jet
remark: The winning element here is the very Ferré masculine/feminine material mix where delicate tone-on-tone beading (tunic) contrasts playfully with a zigzag pattern (coat).
working_process: Allover hand embroidery with small jet beading
llm_description: Sketch of a knee-length skirt and top outfit.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22097.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Tunic in cashmere knit fabric with stitched silk georgette doubling. High neck, long wide tubular sleeves, linear 7/8 cut with metal button fastening on left shoulder. Skirt in two-layer georgette. Ankle length, hip-hugging basque, skinny waistband.
materials: Cashmere knit (Maglificio di Vignola, Vignola), silk georgette, silk print fabric (Sisan, Como; art. 219; des. 941, var. 6)
remark: What makes the outfit distinctive is the opposition between top and bottom, namely between the geometric tunic with a quiet oriental flair and the billowy light skirt with soft pleating.
working_process: Horizontal parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing; flat pleating in various depths
llm_description: A gray tunic and skirt outfit featuring a high-neck, wide-sleeved tunic and a polka-dot, ankle-length skirt with a hip-hugging basque.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/41683.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Caban in dove gray silk organza with cornely embroidery. 7/8 length, kimono sleeves, no collar. T-shirt in dove gray silk chiffon with cornely embroidery. Hip skimming. Skirt in gray wool grisaille fabric. Straight knee-length cut. Sash in gray silk faille with 1 cm parallel topstitching.
materials: Silk organza, wool grisaille (Loro Piana, Quarona Sesia), silk faille, silk chiffon; viscose ribbon
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The elementary geo shapes of jacket and T-shirt find a perfect sense of counterpoint in the gorgeous material with rich embroidery and glossy/matte texture.
working_process: Serpentine & spiral cornely embroidery with viscose ribbon (Ricamificio Damiani)
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a caban, t-shirt, and skirt.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22108.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in black silk gazar. Hip-skimming circular cut with high gloss wool/silk edging. Dress in black silk gazaar with long sleeves and low-cut V neckline. Knee length. Lavish trumpet effect in front thanks to a semi-circular panel with matching cinch belt at waist.
materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829 100% silk; col. black), wool/silk blend fabric (Taroni, Como; art. 17055 66% wool, 27% silk 7% nylon; col. black)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.
llm_description: Black two-piece outfit featuring a hip-skimming jacket with a circular cut and a knee-length dress with a low-cut V neckline, cinched at the waist with a wide belt.

label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in black silk gazar. Hip-skimming circular cut with high gloss wool/silk edging. Dress in black silk gazaar with long sleeves and low-cut V neckline. Knee length. Lavish trumpet effect in front thanks to a semi-circular panel with matching cinch belt at waist.
materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829 100% silk; col. black), wool/silk blend fabric (Taroni, Como; art. 17055 66% wool, 27% silk 7% nylon; col. black)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.
llm_description: Black two-piece outfit featuring a hip-skimming jacket with a circular cut and a knee-length dress with a low-cut V neckline, cinched at the waist with a wide belt.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22115.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Tunica in georgette di seta grigia completamente ricamata; profondo scollo a V; manica lunga a tubo; linea a stringere al fondo; lunghezza 7/8 . Gonna in lana-seta nera; linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio.
materials: Silk georgette (Taroni, Como), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. black), strass and jets in gray, white and anthracite tones
remark: What’s remarkable here is how an item reminiscent of a maxi pullover, semi homewear mode, can go totally glam thanks to beading creating the illusion of a shimmering tweed fabric.
working_process: Allover hand beading with tweedy effect
llm_description: A shimmering blue tunic dress with a deep V-neck and wide sleeves, heavily embellished with sequins, paired with white gloves and a dark headpiece.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22122.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: T-shirt in black breitschwanz with white silk satin collar and black silk satin bow. Skirt in two-layer black silk georgette with hip-hugging basque. Soft flat pleating, deep front slit. Ankle length.
materials: Black breitschwanz fur, silk georgette (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 01031, col. black), silk satin
remark: The unusual use of fur, lusciously soft thanks to a special treatment, takes the concept of ultra simple tee to the next level. Equally interesting is the contrast between superlight georgette fabric and thick rich breitschwanz fur.
working_process: Fine flat pleating with even 3 cm spacing
llm_description: Black breitschwanz fur t-shirt with a white silk satin collar and black silk satin bow, paired with a black silk georgette ankle-length skirt featuring soft flat pleating and a deep front slit.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/48986.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Long dress with black velvet bodice and slim b&w chalkstripe silk cady skirt. Silk taffeta belt complete with decorative topstitching. Stole in chalkstripe cady with black organza on the underside. A stunning wrap construction with all the flair of a giant ribbon.
exhibitions: “Italian Style since 1945”, Victoria and Albert Museum, London, 2015
materials: Black&white chalkstripe print silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 6422, des. 25, col. 1), silk velvet (Redaelli velvet; art. Prestige super; col. black), silk taffeta, silk organza
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Bordering between sleek feminine severity and slinky sari type silhouette, this dress makes a strong impact on both graphic and chromatic levels. Another point of interest is the revisitation in evening version of a supremely mannish chalkstripe pattern.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a dress with a black velvet bodice and a slim black and white chalk stripe skirt, featuring a silk taffeta belt with decorative topstitching and a chalk stripe cady stole with black organza on the underside.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16536.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Ankle-length dress cut at waist with 6-panel bustier whalebone bodice in black & white horizontal pinstripe cady and superslim 4-panel skirt in vertical stripe cady. Medium high slit on left. Silk taffeta ribbon belt with decorative topstitching. Long slender white silk gazar stole with white silk organza lining, softly embracing arms and shoulders.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015 “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, 2015
materials: Black&white chalkstripe print silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 6422, des. 25, col. 1), silk gazar (Taroni, Como; art. 17028, col. 3020), silk taffeta, silk organza
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The sense of illusion is clear: what appears to be an exquisitely simple strapless one-sleeve blouse is actually a stole referencing both Indian sari and Japanese origami. Accentuating the severity elegance further is the use of mannish chalkstripes – ever a Ferré favorite – to create a singular graphic effect essential to the designer’s rethinking of feminine chic. Another must in the Ferré fashion lexicon is the wide ribbon style belt with decorative topstitching and magnificent bow flourish.
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. Lessons in Fashion”, Maria Luisa Frisa (edited by), Marsilio, Venezia, 2009; “Moda. Dalla nascita della haute couture a oggi”, Sofia Gnoli, Carocci Editore, Roma, 2012.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow
llm_description: Fashion illustration of an evening dress featuring a black and white pinstripe skirt and a white top, accompanied by a long white stole.

Accentuating the severity elegance further is the use of mannish chalkstripes – ever a Ferré favorite – to create a singular graphic effect essential to the designer’s rethinking of feminine chic. Another must in the Ferré fashion lexicon is the wide ribbon style belt with decorative topstitching and magnificent bow flourish.
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. Lessons in Fashion”, Maria Luisa Frisa (edited by), Marsilio, Venezia, 2009; “Moda. Dalla nascita della haute couture a oggi”, Sofia Gnoli, Carocci Editore, Roma, 2012.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow
llm_description: Ankle-length evening dress with a black and white pinstripe skirt and a white top with a long white stole, accessorized with a black ribbon belt, gloves, and a hat.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22139.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Cape in ivory silk faille. 7/8 circle cut, capacious sleeves, no collar. Ivory beaver fur lining. Long dress in ivory silk faille. Slim line with two trumpet cut panels in front draping fluidly from shoulder and upper side seams. A wide belt in matching fabric holds the panels softly in place at waist yet only in front.
materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
remark: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan. The sumptuous style is there in the shapes, volumes and proportions. As evident in back, the dress is for the most part slim fit. The ballgown allure comes from the gorgeous material and, best of all, from the two rich paremontures (facings) gently embracing shoulders.
llm_description: Ivory silk faille dress and cape outfit, featuring a long dress with a slim line and trumpet cut panels, paired with a wide matching belt, and a 7/8 circle cut cape with capacious sleeves and beaver fur lining.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/22140.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in boxy shape. Wide sleeves, no collar or closure. Ivory beaver fur lining. Shirt in ivory silk faille. Ample line narrowing gently toward bottom. Long sleeves. Collar and wide lapels held firmly in place at waist, no fastening. Skirt in black silk faille. Long and lavish with double construction: underneath, skinny skirt with three-button front fastening; on top, fully circular one totally open in front.
materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black & col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
remark: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity. Complete with new and interesting tweaks, as in the boxy jacket versus the ballgown skirt.
llm_description: Three-piece evening outfit featuring a boxy ivory jacket with wide sleeves and beaver fur lining, an ivory silk faille shirt, and a long, lavish black silk faille skirt with a skinny underskirt and a fully circular overskirt open in the front.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/studies/35898.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Long dress in black silk faille with trompe-l’oeil construction, namely mimicking a slimline shirtwaist worn backwards. Circular panels starting at shoulders and passing firmly under the cinch belt create an overskirt effect open in front.
materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black, 100% silk)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.
llm_description: Technical sketch of a dress with a slim bodice and a voluminous skirt, featuring circular panels and a belt at the waist.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/60384.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Cape in macro tartan print silk serge with full doubling in same fabric. A-line cut, floor length. Shawl collar, capacious sleeves. Skirt in macro tartan print silk serge. Slim line, floor length, front slit. A herringbone effect in front thanks to the two-section bias cut. Back on the grain in sync with the tartan pattern. Bustier in cotton piqué with allover topstitching for a stifffish feel. Short, hip skimming style. Sash in black silk taffeta.
materials: White&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk); white 100% cotton piqué, pure silk taffeta (col. black)
remark: Capturing the imagination here is the opulent tartan print. The use of this pattern on the bias for the front part of the skirt adds a strong element of graphic contrast to the whole.
working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over bodice and sash
llm_description: Three-piece evening dress featuring a white strapless bustier top, a floor-length skirt with a front slit, and a matching floor-length cape; the skirt and cape are made of macro tartan print silk serge, and the outfit is cinched with a black sash.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/41740.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Top in black velvet with allover topstitching. Sleeveless, (upper) hip skimming. Round neck in front, total rectangular low cut in back. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve.
materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
remark: The architect of fashion’s DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece evening dress featuring a sleeveless top, a floor-length skirt, and a stole, with a macro tartan print on the skirt.

llm_description: Fashion illustration of a three-piece evening dress featuring a tartan skirt, top, and stole.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1986-87 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59609.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Top short and snug, (upper) hip skimming in length. Halter neck, crisscross ribbon straps down to waist in back. Allover topstitching. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back.
materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
remark: Here too the architect of fashion’s DNA emerges equally in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
working_process: Horizonal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top and the stole
llm_description: Three-piece evening dress featuring a black velvet top with halter neck and crisscross ribbon straps, a macro tartan print skirt with a deep front slit, and a stole with a funnel-style sleeve, cinched with a black velvet belt.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40036.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in black grain de poudre wool. High waist, amphora shape. Radial arm construction with narrowing toward lower edge of sleeve. Round tip lapels down to waist. One-button closure. Besom pockets, plus a flap pocket on left. Knee-skimming length. Black silk crepe de chine lining. Skirt in black grain de poudre wool. Four panel construction. Slim line, knee length. Shirt in white tubular silk georgette. Kimono style sleeves narrowing at slit cuff. Mannish silk satin collar in bombé cut. Hip- hugging basque for a blousy effect. Bow in brown silk taffeta. Long and wide (5 cm) with 1 cm parallel topstiching.
materials: Grain de poudre wool (Schontess, Milano; art. 799, col. black), pure silk tubular georgette (Clerici Tessuto, art. 1754, des. 110), silk satin, silk taffeta, silk crepe de chine
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Here’s another outfit capturing to perfection Gianfranco Ferré’s passion for mingling masculine and feminine fashion with thoughtful elegance. The extralong jacket references the 19th century men’s tailcoat, where the new bombé styling in no way took away from the original design of this classic article of bourgeois clothing.
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a jacket, skirt, and shirt with a bow, capturing the silhouette and key design elements.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40042.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: T-shirt in glossy brown crocodile leather. Elbow-length kimono sleeves. Crew neck with opening in back. Pants in brown/white chalkstripe wool. High waist with two tucks. Straight leg, ankle length. Belt in glossy brown crocodile leather with rectangular buckle. Bow in brown/white polkadot chiffon. Lavish proportions with two-button closure. Detachable.
materials: Chalkstripe wool (Schontess), print silk chiffon (Clerici Tessuto, Como), genuine crocodile leather with high gloss finish
remark: What makes this outfit elegantly unique and recherché is the synergistic relationship between feminine chic and mannish style, between fanciful dots and severe stripes.
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a two-piece outfit featuring a brown striped top and pants, paired with a white polkadot scarf.

label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care.

working_process: Applicazioni

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40070.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress bustier style. Form fit, knee length. With silk tape on brown organza silk lining zigzagging across the whole dress. Silk taffeta ribbon on bodice, knotting into a bow complete with big velvet pansy
materials: Silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como), 1 cm wide silk tape, silk taffeta (Clerici Tessuto, Como)

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40088.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress with brown/black macro houndstooth check silk skirt. Slim line, knee length, wrap style closure in back with diagonally overlapping edges. Form-fitting bustier bodice in black lurex lace with truncated cone element (richly embroidered with flowers & spirals) opening on one side to create a corolla effect. Long brown velvet ribbon with 1 cm parallel topstitching.
materials: Silk jacquard (Schoentess, Milano; art. 122, des. 5181, var.1), cotton/lurex lace, silk velvet
remark: Some true hallmarks of the Ferré style confirm the authenticity of this creation: namely, the luxurious materials and the gorgeous details, all at once decorative and structural.
working_process: Complex black silk/lurex embroidery
llm_description: Sketch of a dress with a bustier bodice and a skirt, featuring design notes.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16544.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress redingote style in black cutwork silk crêpe with black silk satin lining. Slim bodice with little lapel collar, padded shoulders, long narrow sleeves. Front metal-clasp closure with small overlay. Skirt with lovely A-line flare. Under the collar are big black organza & velvet leaf appliqués, with matching topstitched silk satin ribbon to knot below. Black organza lining for top part of the dress. Then for added fullness, the flaring skirt has a big nylon flounce on the inside.
exhibitions: “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
materials: Silk crepe, silk satin, silk organza, cotton velvet, nylon
remark: Here’s a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man’s redingote. Distinguishing features include the silk crêpe fabric complete with meticulous cutwork: the classic eyelet effect is the amazing result of hand craftsmanship performed on the finished garment.
working_process: Silk fabric all in cutwork motif
llm_description: Sketch of a dress redingote with a slim bodice and A-line skirt, featuring a little lapel collar and padded shoulders.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40098.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Coat-dress in black silk marocain. Six-part construction, amphora line, ankle length. Kimono style sleeves narrowing toward slit lower edge (to turn up in a cuff). Bombé-cut lapels & mid bottom opening. Black crepe de chine lining. Two black silk taffeta ribbons (20 cm wide) with 1 cm parallel topstitching at sides of upper back knot into a bow.
materials: Silk marocain (Taroni, Como; art. 17028), silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. black
remark: While an exquisite expression of severity elegance, this dress holds elements of surprise: the sumptuous decor at right wrist level and the big bow at back waist (totally invisible from the front).
working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Sketch of a coat dress with an amphora line, featuring a large bow at the back waist.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16545.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress in brown silk taffeta with decorative topstitching. Slim strapless bodice in double layer of silk faille (30 cm wide ribbon) crisscrossing neatly in front and then knotting obi style in back. Under the bustline a circular panel of topstitched silk taffeta flares beautifully outward from large fluid pleats.
materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 3057, var. 824), silk faille (Clerici Tessuto; Como), nylon
remark: This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in sublime opposition to one another.
working_process: 1 cm. parallel topstitching all along the circular panel
llm_description: Fashion sketch of a dress featuring a strapless bodice and a flared skirt, with linear details suggesting fabric texture and movement.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16541.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
description: Abito – bustier composto da più elementi che si sovrappongono: il bustino è formato da due tronchi di cono che si aprono a corolla sul davanti; la parte inferiore è costituita da una gonna a ¾ di ruota su cui è cucito un elemento a mezza ruota a formare pieghe. La crinolina di nylon inserita nei profili esterni sostiene le ampiezze e favorisce un effetto plastico e tridimensionale.
materials: Taffeta di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como art. 0802 - col. nero) trapunto e matelassé; duchesse di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como); crinolina di nylon.
remark: Tutto si arrotonda, tutto si dilata. Il capo ondeggia nella sua ruota quasi conica con un movimento fermato nel tempo, in perfetto equilibrio di volumi e proporzioni.
working_process: impunture parallele a mm10 seguono l’andamento delle forme geometriche del capo
llm_description: Sketch of a dress with a bustier top and a full, layered skirt, rendered with curved lines to indicate volume and movement.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40116.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress ballgown style in silk faille with giant stripes (black, white, fuchsia, silver). Slim strapless bodice with low-cut V neckline in back. Voluminous skirt thanks to large fluid pleats. Brown silk floral & spiral embroidery on wide black band at hip level. Black silk gazar lining. Skirt (underneath) in brown silk velvet with 1 cm topstitching. Mid-thigh length. Knotted black silk faille ribbon on waistband.
materials: Silk faille in wide bands of color (Clerici Tessuto, Como; des. 2, var. 2), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como; col. brown)
remark: The outstanding feature here is the artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions, so revealing once again the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how in back the large skirt rests on the short slim one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape.
working_process: Complex brown silk relief embroidery; parallel topstiching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Sketch of a strapless ballgown with a slim bodice and voluminous skirt, featuring giant stripes and floral embroidery at the hip level.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/40118.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress with a bustier made of white cotton honeycomb piqué and a skirt in purple silk faille with black and gold stripes, with a satin weave that does not appear on the reverse side.The panels are assembled with large loose pleats in the skirt and are hollowed out from the waist up. Black silk organza lining. A topstitched satin ribbon at 0.5 cm is attached to the dress, tied in a bow at the front, over which the skirt drapes to form a drape. Nylon crinoline structure supports the shape and weight of the skirt. Brown velvet underskirt; fitted; mid-thigh length.
materials: White piqué; purple silk faille with black and gold stripes (Clerici Tessuto, Como; design 3 var.4); brown silk velvet (Stucchi, Como); black silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como); nylon crinoline.
present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
remark: Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how the long full skirt rests on the short one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape. With additional support from the big satiny silk bow.
working_process: Parallel topstitching at 0.5 cm, embroidery.
llm_description: Sketch of a two-piece evening dress featuring a bustier top and a full skirt with pleats and a large bow at the waist.

present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
remark: Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how the long full skirt rests on the short one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape. With additional support from the big satiny silk bow.
working_process: Parallel topstitching at 0.5 cm, embroidery.
llm_description: A two-piece evening dress featuring a white bustier top, a brown bodice, and a purple silk faille skirt with black and gold stripes, accented by a large gold bow at the waist and paired with lilac gloves.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1987-88 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16546.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Dress in black wool crepe with eight-part construction. Long lavishly flaring skirt with asymmetric front hem. Underneath, black nylon crinoline petticoat with three gold lamé plisse flounces. Long bodice with eight-part construction. Round neck, narrow wrist-length sleeves.
materials: Wool crepe (Schontess, Milano), black nylon, gold lurex lace, gold sequins
remark: A dark lady of past times. Here the chaste severity of the Victorian Age is offset by quiet elements of wicked femininity, as in the gold flounces with gilt sequins peeking out from under the skirt’s asymmetric hemline.
working_process: Sunburst pleating on petticoat flounces; sequining
llm_description: Fashion sketch of a floor-length dress with a fitted bodice and a lavishly flaring skirt, featuring a round neck and wrist-length sleeves.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35715.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Coat with round neckline and wide ¾ sleeves. Soft A line, knee length. Dress crewneck tube style with short wide sleeves. Above-the-knee length. Upper section in black velvet, with essential horizontal cut across top of bustline; main section in gray melange wool flannel with six-part vertical construction.
materials: Silver fox fur (Mondialpelli, Milano), double alpaca wool fabric (Agnona,Biella; art. 10876, var. M65), wool flannel (Lanapon; art. 13702, var.130), cotton velvet (Redaelli velvets, Mandello del Lario; art. prestige super black
remark: From the strong shoulders to the gentle flaring all the way to the knee-skimming length, this ensemble revisits and renders homage to iconic 1930s and ‘40s Hollywood and Cinecittà (a.k.a. Hollywood on the Tiber) glamour. The silver fox with alpaca intarsiawork makes for a wonderfully light & fluffy sensation.
working_process: Horizontal band intarsiawork in silver fox and double alpaca
llm_description: Sketch of a coat with a round neckline and wide sleeves.

acquisition: Acquisto asta Il Ponte novembre 2022 Open Care, Stand 32
source: Cartella stampa, Collezione Alta Moda Autunno-Inverno 1988/89
description: Cappotto lunghezza al ginocchio, maniche a kimono tagliate, girocollo, chiusura con due bottoni rivestiti, foderato in crêpe de chine. Il capo è costruito da 5 teli cuciti ad intarsio tra loro, seguendo il motivo a volute. Il capo è completamente ricamato con fili diversi. I motivi a volute disegnano anche i davanti, il fondo del cappotto e il fondo maniche. Ulteriormente ricamato con fori che seguono il disegno.
materials: Lana mohair ricamata (Agnona, art.12517, var.M65), crêpe de chine grigio scuro.
remark: Si denota il fascino di Ferré per il Romanticismo sensuale e misterioso dell’Ottocento. Reso noto dal disegno del ricamo con un motivo ad ampie volute che riprendono moduli organici e scultorei che si sommano in un vortice roteante e sospeso, generando forme che plasmano il cappotto di lana.
working_process: Ricamo a mano, in filo di lana grigio mélange, nero e filo di seta grigio perla; traforature su tutto il capo rinforzate con filato grigio; cuciture a intarsio.
llm_description: Fabric swatches including gray wool with a swirl design and a gray floral print.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/48994.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in wool flannel with thick allover wool/viscose spiral & circle embroidery. Slim hip-skimming line with round neck, long sleeves, no fastening. Extensive cord passementerie in unevenly meandering pattern, held in place here & there by wooden rings with braid covering. Tassles big and small sewn on so as to create an asymmetric effect. Shirt in wool/silk print fabric with straight line, back closure. Small Peter Pan style collar with high neckband support element. Skirt all in horizontal bands of glossy python leather. Slim fit, knee length. Belt in python leather with matching buckle.
materials: Wool flannel (Lanapon, art.13702/348A), print wool/silk (Sisan, Milano; art.1095, des.1538), python leather, viscose cord passementerie, wool/viscose embroidery thread
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The embroidery on the jacket – where the gracious reference to Pompier art is clear – sparks fond memories of the European salon lifestyle of the late 19th century. Drapery type tassles & cords define the ornamentation on an item essentially modern of cut, while also creating a sense of contrast with the (equally simple of shape) snakeskin skirt. The latter is precious in an aggressive way, thanks to the use of high-tech processes in delineating a new narrative for the basic material.
working_process: Wool/viscose cornely (machine) embroidery, hand application of tassles plus viscose cord in three different sizes
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a jacket with cord passementerie, a shirt with a Peter Pan collar, and a skirt.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35727.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: Giacca girocollo, senza chiusure; maniche lunghe; linea asciutta; lunghezza ai fianchi. Il tessuto di lana jacquard a disegno paisley è piazzato a scialli. I colori di base di tessitura sono bruciato, bordeaux,ocra e marrone. Il nero è creato da un sovraricamo a mano a punto catenella. Profilo davanti in zibellino tinto bordeaux. Gonna a vita alta, molto aderente; lunghezza al ginocchio. Materiale e ricamo come la giacca. Camicia di linea diritta, con chiusura posteriore, in raso di seta bordeaux, con piccolo colletto a punta stondata, sostenuto da un’alta pistagna. Cache col ad effetto drappeggio irregolare, ottenuto con passaggi di fili che trattengono dall’interno il tessuto. Scialle quadrato con corta sfrangiatura ai lati.
exhibitions: “Cachemire. Il Segno in movimento”, Fondazione Antonio Ratti, Villa Sucota/Como e Villa Bernasconi/Cernobbio, 2016
materials: Lana jacquard a disegno cachemire (Etro, Milano art. 639 var. 4); raso di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como art. 801 var. 874); zibellino (Montorsi,Bologna).
remark: La definizione formale di grande rigore del completo si oppone ed allo stesso tempo si compenetra dello sfarzo derivato dai colori caldi, ma soprattutto dal ricorso, volutamente opulento, ai motivi paisley: questi sono divenuti di gran moda in Occidente a partire dal secolo XIX, quando il fascino esercitato dal gusto decorativo orientale-subcontinentale indiano, in special modo, coincide cronologicamente con l’introduzione dei telai jacquard in area inglese e scozzese.
working_process: Ricamo a mano, punto catenella in filo nero, piazzato su disegno paisley jacquard.
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a jacket, top, skirt, and scarf.

remark: La definizione formale di grande rigore del completo si oppone ed allo stesso tempo si compenetra dello sfarzo derivato dai colori caldi, ma soprattutto dal ricorso, volutamente opulento, ai motivi paisley: questi sono divenuti di gran moda in Occidente a partire dal secolo XIX, quando il fascino esercitato dal gusto decorativo orientale-subcontinentale indiano, in special modo, coincide cronologicamente con l’introduzione dei telai jacquard in area inglese e scozzese.
working_process: Ricamo a mano, punto catenella in filo nero, piazzato su disegno paisley jacquard.
llm_description: A three-piece outfit featuring a paisley jacquard jacket with a sable fur trim, a matching skirt, and a silk satin shirt with a draped scarf; the jacket has long sleeves and a collarless neckline.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59690.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: Giacca girocollo, senza chiusure; maniche lunghe; linea asciutta; lunghezza ai fianchi. Il tessuto di lana jacquard a disegno paisley è piazzato a scialli. I colori di base di tessitura sono bruciato, bordeaux,ocra e marrone. Il nero è creato da un sovraricamo a mano a punto catenella. Profilo davanti in zibellino tinto bordeaux. Gonna a vita alta, molto aderente; lunghezza al ginocchio. Materiale e ricamo come la giacca. Camicia di linea diritta, con chiusura posteriore, in raso di seta bordeaux, con piccolo colletto a punta stondata, sostenuto da un’alta pistagna. Cache col ad effetto drappeggio irregolare, ottenuto con passaggi di fili che trattengono dall’interno il tessuto. Scialle quadrato con corta sfrangiatura ai lati.
exhibitions: “Cachemire. Il Segno in movimento”, Fondazione Antonio Ratti, Villa Sucota/Como e Villa Bernasconi/Cernobbio, 2016

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35728.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: Cappotto di taglio maschile, dalla linea asciutta; monopetto a due bottoni; revers a lancia; lunghezza al ginocchio. Il tessuto di lana jacquard a disegno paisley è piazzato a scialli. I colori di base di tessitura sono bruciato, bordeaux, ocra e marrone. Il nero è creato da un sovraricamo a mano a punto catenella. Camicia di linea diritta, con chiusura posteriore, in raso di seta bordeaux, con piccolo colletto a punta stondata, sostenuto da una alta pistagna. Gonna di linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio; realizzata con 8 teli verticali in panno di lana nero; apertura con zip sul dietro. Cache-col ad effetto drappeggio irregolare, ottenuto con passaggi di fili che trattengono dall’interno il tessuto.
exhibitions: “Cachemire. Il Segno in movimento” , Fondazione Antonio Ratti, Villa Sucota/Como e Villa Bernasconi/Cernobbio, 2016
materials: Lana jacquard a disegno cachemire (Etro, Milano; art. 639 var. 4); raso di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 801 var. 874); panno di lana.
remark: Di taglio squisitamente maschile e austero: un severo chesterfield monopetto. Il completo risulta decisamente femminilizzato dal cache- col mosso ed enfatizzato dal drappeggio, dalla calda sensualità delle tonalità, dalla raffinatezza dei sovraricami e dai macro-motivi, ricorrenti anche nei tessuti da arredamento del secondo scorcio del XIX secolo.
working_process: Ricamo a mano punto catenella in filo nero, piazzato su disegno paisley jacquard.
llm_description: Sketch of an outfit including a coat, skirt, shirt, and scarf.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35731.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
description: Giacca di taglio maschile, monopetto a un bottone; revers a lancia;linea asciutta; lunghezza al fianco; maniche molto lunghe da tenere arricciate. Il capo è in raso di lana e seta stampato a disegno paisley, piazzato a foulard con cornice in tinta unita nera. Colori: bruciato, ocra,bordeaux, rosso e nero. Canotta di linea diritta in raso di seta stampato a disegni paisley (colori come giacca) girocollo, senza maniche. Pantaloni in velluto di seta stampa paisley (colori come giacca). Linea morbida creata da due pieghe che partono dalla cintura; vita alta; chiusura frontale con zip invisibile e bottone a vista. Scialle quadrato imbottito e impunturato: un lato in raso di seta, stampa a disegno paisley piazzato a foulard e l’altro in velluto di seta con stampa all over. Trapuntatura a disegno floreale .
materials: raso di lana e seta stampa paisley (Ratti Donna, Como art.097/21 dis. F28501 e art. 0105/121 dis. 28501); velluto di seta stampa paisley (Ratti Donna Como art. velluto /21 dis. 59465)
remark: All’asciuttezza della linea della giacca-blazer fanno da contrasto sia le maniche dalla lunghezza esasperata, che necessariamente genera arricciature, sia l’ampiezza dei pantaloni, sostanzialmente estranea ai canoni occidentali. Il motivo paisley, comune a giacca, pantaloni e scialle, assume luci diverse a causa di materiali e lavorazioni diverse nei tre pezzi.
working_process: Imbottitura e trapuntatura di raso e velluto a stampa paisley abbinati.
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a jacket, top, pants, and shawl.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/35743.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Boxy long-sleeve crewneck 9/10th coat, buttonless. In silk organza with allover application of handcrafted white & black silk jacquard rosebuds, plus inclusion against this thick floral backdrop of feather-tipped black silk organza stalks. Slim body-hugging sleeveless high-neck tube dress, knee length. In silk organza with allover horizontal application of passementerie. This ribbon trim is sewn on with no interruptions, so to allow for the mid-back zip closure it simply switches direction and keeps on going.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
materials: White & black birdseye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como art. 5384/4001 var. 000/4001), silk organza, high sheen “salt & pepper” viscose passementerie
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: Reproducing the structural identity of 1940s furs, the uniqueness of both materials and working processes heightens the impact of volumes and gives the coat a special sensation of impalpable lightness. Expressly contrasting the coat's rich proportions is the slinkiness of the figure-flattering dress, second skin mode. Complete with playful glints and glossy tweed effects. All clearly strong on glamorous allure.
working_process: Two allover applications: one of small handcrafted silk flowers; the other of passementerie, soutache style, sewn on horizontally non-stop (no cuts)
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a coat and dress ensemble, featuring a boxy, long-sleeve coat with allover application of handcrafted white and black silk jacquard rosebuds, and a slim, body-hugging, sleeveless, high-neck tube dress with allover horizontal application of passementerie.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59733.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Boxy long-sleeve crewneck 9/10th coat, buttonless. In silk organza with allover application of handcrafted white & black silk jacquard rosebuds, plus inclusion against this thick floral backdrop of feather-tipped black silk organza stalks. Slim body-hugging sleeveless high-neck tube dress, knee length. In silk organza with allover horizontal application of passementerie. exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
materials: White & black birdseye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como art. 5384/4001 var. 000/4001), silk organza, high sheen “salt & pepper” viscose passementerie

remark: Reproducing the structural identity of 1940s furs, the uniqueness of both materials and working processes heightens the impact of volumes and gives the coat a special sensation of impalpable lightness. Expressly contrasting the coat's rich proportions is the slinkiness of the figure-flattering dress, second skin mode. Complete with playful glints and glossy tweed effects. source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59735.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in white&black micro bird’s-eye silk cady. Short long-sleeve spencer cut. Lavish ripple/spiral salt&pepper silk ribbon embroidery in both flat and twofold appliqué form. Rosebuds in the same basic silk cady all along bottom hem and at wide lower edge of sleeves (diminishing upward here). Dress in black silk velvet. Short, sleeveless, low-cut rectangular neckline. Amphora shape. At midriff, black cady band with horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching. Gentle gathering at waist.
materials: White&black bird’s-eye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como; art. 5384/4001, var. 000/4001), silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk velvet (Redaelli; art. Ivonne super)
remark: While essentially slim of silhouette, this outfit features an exquisite opulence deriving from the recherché decorative elements – all the richer at waist and wrist level. The short little jacket comes across as a modern-day take on the ones that back in the days of the Second Empire (1852-1870) Charles Worth combined with crinoline style skirts (so giving origin to the contemporary woman’s suit).
working_process: Soutage & cornely style embroidery of narrow passementerie ribbon; application of small handmade silk flowers
llm_description: Two-piece outfit featuring a short jacket in a bird's-eye pattern with lavish ribbon embroidery and rosebud detailing, paired with a black silk velvet dress with a rectangular neckline and a cady band at the midriff.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33195.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré (gold lettering)
description: Jacket in white&black prince of wales (glen plaid) wool with thick allover embroidery/beading. Short boxy cut. Long sleeves, no fastening. Lavish bas-relief design thanks to mix of different techniques & materials. Natural chinchilla trim. Pants in white&black prince of wales wool. Slim mannish style with tucks, pressed pleat, slant pockets. Ankle length. Bustier top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Vertical construction, (upper) hip skimming. Cummerbund in black silk cady.
materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), wool (Fila, Biella; art. 16200, var. 4211), chinchilla fur (Mondialpelli, Milano), glass tube beads-crystals-paillettes
remark: Thanks to opulent materials (dazzling crystals, fabulous fur) and artful embroidery/intarsiawork, the classic jacket is all set to hit the Gran Galà scene. The match with ultra mannish glen plaid pants is equally innovative, makes for an authentic conversation piece in the realm of couture.
working_process: Hand embroidery with silver glass tube beads, paillettes, rosette- and teardrop-cut diamond crystals in chrome settings
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece outfit including a jacket, bustier top, and pants.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59768.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Top in short, slim, sleeveless cut. With round neckline. Skirt in white&black Vichy wool with rich allover embroidery. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Lavish bas-relief design thanks to mix of different techniques & materials.
materials: White&black Vichy design wool (Fila, Biella; art. 16200, var. 4201)

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33171.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Gala evening coat in velvet and peau d’ange color taffeta with fine layer of padding in between. Shawl collar, wide sleeves. Trumpet line with asymmetric bombé hem forming a small train in back. Parallel topstitching in snyc with the circular construction links the three black materials. Dress sleeveless tube style, knee length. Color: black. Vertical construction with allover horizontal 1 cm parallel topstiching.
materials: Silk taffeta (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 802, var. 1001), silk velvet (Pernet velours, Paris; art. 1112 /111 var 900)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The grandeur of this evening cloak enfolds in perfect unison references to typical 1950s shapes & proportions (re: American/European fashion), to the Spanish “manton de torero” and to the Japanese uchikake wedding kimono. Absolutely by design, the cape’s hieratic sumptuousness steals the scene from the exquisitely simple dress underneath.
working_process: Allover parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: Fashion illustration of an evening outfit featuring a coat with wide sleeves and a dress, both adorned with parallel topstitching.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59794.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Gala evening coat in velvet and peau d’ange color taffeta with fine layer of padding in between. Shawl collar, wide sleeves. Trumpet line with asymmetric bombé hem forming a small train in back. Parallel topstitching in snyc with the circular construction links the three black materials. Dress sleeveless tube style, knee length. Color: black. Vertical construction with allover horizontal 1 cm parallel topstiching.
materials: Silk taffeta (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 802, var. 1001), silk velvet (Pernet velours, Paris; art. 1112 /111 var 900)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: The grandeur of this evening cloak enfolds in perfect unison references to typical 1950s shapes & proportions (re: American/European fashion), to the Spanish “manton de torero” and to the Japanese uchikake wedding kimono. Absolutely by design, the cape’s hieratic sumptuousness steals the scene from the exquisitely simple dress underneath.
working_process: Allover parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
llm_description: A model wears a pink coat with black velvet shawl collar and cuffs, over a black dress, accessorized with a pearl necklace and black gloves.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33173.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: T-shirt in long slim line. Sleeveless crewneck style with fine luminous effect thanks to allover garnet red crystal beading. Skirt in straight line, knee length. Necklace & bracelets torchon mode. All in garnet red crystal beads. Gloves in garnet red suede with matching silk rose appliqués.
materials: Wine red silk georgette, garnet red crystal beads
remark: Captivating femininity takes shape on both conceptual and material levels through this shimmering jewel dress in a warm and sensual shade of red. As for the accessories, from torchon jewelry to floral-decor gloves, they are an integral part of the outfit.
working_process: Allover crystal beading (by hand) in vertical rows
llm_description: Sketch of a two-piece outfit featuring a sleeveless top and skirt, both adorned with a pattern of dots.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show photos/59812.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Shirt in semi-sheer white silk gazar with extrawide lapel collar. Double breasting, two-button closure at waist. Asymmetric paisley/floral embroidery mimicking lace. Skirt in black silk cady. Slim line, high waist, ankle length. Deep slit at mid back. Belt with buckle in matching fabric. Stole long and rectangular of shape. In chiné silk taffeta with degradé dark red rose design on a black base. Black silk gazar lining, dark red rose appliqués.
materials: White silk gazar (Taroni, Como), black silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), chiné à la chaine silk taffeta (Verga, Como; art 3400, var. 40, des. 16872
remark: Of particular interest is the chiné à la chaine silk (re stole) involving a special dye process where small groups of warp threads are tied in such a way so that they remain color free. The warp is then set on the loom and the weaving takes place with a monochromatic weft. The little shifts that the threads undergo in the curling and tying of the warp create a vertical degradé flame effect.
working_process: White-on-white silk embroidery (by hand) complete with rich relief effect
llm_description: Two-piece evening outfit featuring a floral print shawl-like top with a wide white lapel and a black, ankle-length skirt; the stole is cinched at the waist with a large red rose embellishment.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16553.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Black silk gazaar dress with short bustier bodice and trumpet skirt. Two long asymmetric panels at back V neckline draping gently, forming a train. Silk gazar stole hanky style, folded into a triangle, with giant lapel collar attached to it cutting horizontally across the décolleté. Dark red silk velvet & organza rose vine-shoot decor adding to the beauty in back.
exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015, “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, var. black)
present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
remark: While filmmaker Luchino Visconti’s masterpiece “Senso” inspires one part of the collection, this dress captures all the allure of opulent Hapsburgian fashion during the Romantic period. Offsetting the sumptuousness is the modern severity elegance of both monochrome black hue and precision-perfect asymmetry.
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. Lessons in Fasion”, Maria Luisa Frisa (edited by), Marsilio, Venezia, 2009; “The history of modern fashion”, Daniel James Cole and Nancy Deihl, Laurence King Publishing, London, 2015
llm_description: Sketch of an evening dress with a bustier bodice and trumpet skirt, featuring asymmetric panels at the back neckline and a stole with a large lapel collar.

Offsetting the sumptuousness is the modern severity elegance of both monochrome black hue and precision-perfect asymmetry.
bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. Lessons in Fasion”, Maria Luisa Frisa (edited by), Marsilio, Venezia, 2009; “The history of modern fashion”, Daniel James Cole and Nancy Deihl, Laurence King Publishing, London, 2015

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33187.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Ball gown consisting of a shaped nylon crinoline skirt, gathered and scalloped at the hem, and then machine embroidered with a pattern to imitate white lace. Starting from the waist of the skirt, 9 tiered pleated nylon tulle flounces are applied at different heights. Over this structure is placed an overskirt composed of two circular skirts: the innermost of ivory-colored mechanical Chantilly lace, and the outermost of black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes. Floor length. Separate underskirt composed of a black silk satin pencil skirt, fully doubled (not lined). Belt in black silk grosgrain with covered buckle. Fitted black silk velvet top; edges and straps in black silk tubing; length to the high hip. Square shawl in black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes, hand-hemmed.
exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
materials: Black silk gazar with white stripes (Taroni, Como; art. 9967 var. A); black nylon tulle, various weights; ecru silk Chantilly lace (Moda e guarnizioni, Milan; art. 9111); nylon crinoline; silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, Mandello Lario; art. Yvonne super 54902); black silk satin; black silk grosgrain.
present_location: spazio FGF c/o Open Care
remark: A main point of interest here is the use of a mannish pinstripe pattern to counterbalance both the magnificence of the delicately voluminous 19th century ballgown style and the exquisite femininity of a skirt complete with beguiling glimpse of the precious layers underneath.
working_process: Sunburst pleating; machine embroidery with white thread on black tulle with a lace effect.
llm_description: Sketch of a three-piece evening dress featuring a pinstriped shawl, fitted top, and a voluminous skirt with tiered flounces.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33188.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Ballgown skirt circular style, floor length. In multiple layers (each one lifted in front and attached to waist): black plisse tulle for bottom layers, embroidered Chantilly lace for top ones. Belt in fabric with matching buckle. Top in black silk velvet. Short slim line. Spaghetti straps & piping in black tubular silk. Shawl in black lace with jet/paillette embroidery. Boxy shape, handsewn hem.

remark: This dress too is very much about the long and lavishly full skirt with plisse petticoats underneath.
working_process: Hand jet/paillette embroidery on lace; sunburst pleating for the tulle
llm_description: Sketch of a ballgown with a fitted bodice and a voluminous, multi-layered skirt; the design includes a shawl and underskirt.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/33189.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Ballgown skirt circular style, floor length. In multiple layers (each one lifted in front and attached to waist): black tulle with sunburst pleats; nylon with garland-stitch hem; on the exterior, silk gazar with wide smocked ruffle. Belt in fabric with matching buckle. Top in ivory silk duchesse. Short slim line. Spaghetti straps & piping in similar tubular silk. Shawl in silk organza with partial paisley print plus lavish paillette/jet embroidery. Boxy shape.
materials: Silk gazar (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1657), nylon cloth, nylon tulle, silk duchesse (Taroni, Como; art. 1324, var. 908), silk organza with partial paisley print (Ratti, Como), paillettes and jets
remark: Here the paisley design that comprises the collection’s primary theme makes a strong statement in the form of a gala evening accessory.
working_process: Black smocking; black thread embroidery in ringlets and uneven garland stitch; sunburst pleating
llm_description: Sketch of a ballgown featuring a layered, floor-length skirt with ruffles and pleats, paired with a strapless top and a paisley-printed shawl with embellishments.

source_path: Dataset DataShack 2026/ALTA MODA 1988-89 FW/Womenswear/Fashion show drawings/16552.jpg
label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
description: Ballgown skirt voluminous trumpet style, floor length. Exterior layer in six silk print foulards, joined together intarsia mode to form an uninterrupted pattern. Ditto for bustier bodice. Underneath, multiple circular black plisse tulle petticoats (each lifted in front and attached to waist) with print organza border. Shawl in paisley print silk organza. Boxy shape.
materials: Paisley print silk organza (Ratti, Como; colors: ocher, wine, rust, ivory, blue, black), black nylon tulle, lace ribbon, black silk organza, paillettes and jets
remark: What’s striking here is the dual use – namely, both structural and decorative – of the print foulard intarsiawork.
working_process: Jet/paillette encrustation of print fabric; silk foulard intarsiawork; paisley design embroidery; sunburst pleating
llm_description: Fashion illustration of a strapless dress with a full, pleated skirt and a shawl, featuring embellishments and a boxy silhouette.
